body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Major refactor to clean up:
* Simplifies logic of orbital geometry calculation
* Streamlines several calculations to avoid redundancy (e.g., only compute the equation-of-time hour angle correction as an angle, rather than computing an angle, converting to time, then converting back to angle).
* Simplifies external API through use of multiple dispatch (e.g., several methods are provided for an `insolation` function, which should cover most external use cases). Internal helper quantities such as the hour angle at the prime meridian, currently exported to ClimaAtmos, should not be externalized; with this refactor, they won't be.
* Expanded tests
* Provided hook for inclusion of solar luminosity variations (to address #37 eventually)

Closes #78 


## To-do
Breaking Changes
* API changed; previous reference to `instantaneous_zenith...` and `helper...` in ClimaAtmos, ClimaLand, and ClimaCoupler will need to be updated.

## Review
* Review correctness and logic: @Julians42, @szy21 
* Review GPU compatibility (e.g., is `datetime2julian` working correctly on GPUs?): @ph-kev 

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->


<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/
- followed the style guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/DevDocs/CodeStyle/
- followed the documentation policy: https://github.com/CliMA/policies/wiki/Documentation-Policy
- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included 
- relevant unit tests, and integration tests, 
- appropriate docstrings on all functions, structs, and modules, and included relevant documentation.

-->

----
- [ ] I have read and checked the items on the review checklist.
title: Insolation cleanup
